L. 115–271

children.text: mobilizes resources within and outside of the recovery community, which may include through a peer support network, to increase the prevalence and quality of long-term recovery from substance use disorders; and
is wholly or principally governed by people in recovery for substance use disorders who reflect the community served.

children.heading: Grants authorized
children.label: b Grants authorized
children.text: The Secretary shall award grants to recovery community organizations to enable such organizations to develop, expand, and enhance recovery services.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: c Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of the costs of a program funded by a grant under this section may not exceed 85 percent.
shall be used to develop, expand, and enhance community and statewide recovery support services; and

children.label: vii
children.text: other recovery support services that facilitate recovery from substance use disorders, including non-clinical community services;

children.label: B
children.text: reduce stigma associated with substance use disorders; and

children.label: i
children.text: identifying the signs of substance use disorder;

children.label: ii
children.text: the resources available to individuals with substance use disorder and to families of an individual with a substance use disorder, including programs that mentor and provide support services to children;

children.label: iii
children.text: the resources available to help support individuals in recovery; and

children.label: iv
children.text: related medical outcomes of substance use disorders, the potential of acquiring an infection commonly associated with illicit drug use, and neonatal abstinence syndrome among infants exposed to opioids during pregnancy.

children.heading: Special consideration
children.label: e Special consideration
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary shall give special consideration to the unique needs of rural areas, including areas with an age-adjusted rate of drug overdose deaths that is above the national average and areas with a shortage of prevention and treatment services.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $17,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2026 through 2030.
